headline: Is It Too Late To Consider S&P Global (SPGI) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
summary: For readers wondering whether S&P Global at around US$426 per share still offers value or whether most of the opportunity is already reflected in the price, this article walks through that question step by step. The stock has been relatively quiet over the past week with a 0.5% decline, while the 4.2% gain over 30 days sits alongside a 16.8% year to date decline and a 13.2% decline over the past year. Investors have been reacting to ongoing discussion around interest rates, credit conditions...

headline: Nobel Prize Winner Maria Corina Machado to Address CERAWeek by S&P Global in Houston, March 23-27
summary: Nobel Laureate Maria Corina Machado will address delegates at the 44th annual CERAWeek by S&P Global, March 23-27 in Houston. She will join the world's energy industry leaders, experts, government officials and policymakers, as well as leaders from the technology, financial and industrial communities addressing this year's conference.

headline: S&P revises Cliffwater fund outlook to negative on redemption liquidity pressure
summary: S&P Global Ratings affirmed the ‘A’ long-term issuer credit rating for Cliffwater Corporate Lending Fund (CCLF) but revised its outlook to negative, citing pressures on liquidity from elevated redemption requests. The fund repurchased 7% of shares outstanding in Q1, the maximum amount permitted without changing the terms of its repurchase offer and above the minimum quarterly repurchase offer of 5%. headline: BDC Software Loan Payment-in-Kind Rate Rose Slightly in Third Quarter
summary: The ratings firm looked at $146 billion of business development companies and interval funds in technology, finding that 15.3% were paid with new debt instead of money, an approach known as payment-in-kind, in the third quarter of last year.  Concerns that new artificial intelligence products will pressure older software businesses and lead them to default on their loans have rippled through markets this year, driving down stocks for business development companies and private asset managers.
